skill.name: liquidity-depth-analyzer
skill.description: Guides DEX liquidity checks, price impact estimates, slippage limits, and trade abort decisions for swap execution.
skill.summary: Analyze DEX liquidity depth and slippage before executing swaps.
skill.author: BarisSozen

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged one external command pattern and two weak cryptographic algorithm patterns, but all three are false positives after context review. The cited external command location is a TypeScript configuration example, and the cited weak-crypto lines contain descriptive trading text with no cryptographic code.

low_findings.title: False Positive: External Command Pattern
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged Ruby or shell backtick execution at SKILL.md:46. The cited line starts a fenced TypeScript code block for static configuration values, and no command execution instruction is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited block contains a const config object with numeric limits only. No shell command, backtick execution expression, or user-controlled input path appears there.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged a weak cryptographic algorithm at SKILL.md:3. The cited line is the skill description for DEX liquidity analysis and contains no cryptographic primitive or hashing operation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 is metadata text describing the skill purpose. I found no evidence of MD5, SHA-1, DES, RC4, or any cryptographic implementation on that line.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged a weak cryptographic algorithm at SKILL.md:14. The cited line lists position sizing as a use case and contains no cryptographic operation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 14 is plain workflow text. I found no evidence of a weak algorithm, key handling, encryption, hashing, or authentication logic.

content.value_statement: DEX trades can fail or lose value when liquidity depth and slippage are not checked first. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through depth checks, price impact estimates, and abort rules.
actual_capabilities: Checks whether available depth is at least three times the planned trade size.
Calculates price impact in basis points using spot and execution prices.
Computes minimum output amounts from expected output and slippage tolerance.
Applies abort reasons for missing pools, low depth, high impact, and low profit.
Supports reasoning about swaps, route selection, and position sizing.
limitations: Does not fetch live pool data or connect to blockchain nodes.
Does not execute trades or sign transactions.
Requires accurate pool reserves, quotes, or route data from the user.
Does not replace professional financial or risk advice.

use_cases.title: Pre-trade swap review
use_cases.description: Review a planned DEX swap for liquidity depth, expected price impact, and slippage tolerance before execution.

use_cases.title: Route selection validation
use_cases.description: Compare candidate swap routes and identify routes that fail depth or price impact limits.

use_cases.title: Arbitrage profit screening
use_cases.description: Check whether expected arbitrage profit survives slippage, fees, and liquidity constraints.

prompt_templates.title: Basic depth check
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of one planned swap.
prompt_templates.prompt: Check this DEX trade for liquidity depth. Trade size: [amount]. Available depth: [depth]. Tell me if it passes the 3x rule.

prompt_templates.title: Slippage estimate
prompt_templates.scenario: Estimate minimum output for one swap.
prompt_templates.prompt: Estimate the minimum amount out for this swap. Expected output: [amount]. Slippage tolerance: [bps]. Show the formula and result.

prompt_templates.title: Route comparison
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare these DEX routes for liquidity, price impact, and slippage risk: [route data]. Recommend which routes to keep or reject.

prompt_templates.title: Arbitrage validation
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced profit check with abort reasoning.
prompt_templates.prompt: Validate this arbitrage opportunity after liquidity, slippage, price impact, and fees. Data: [quotes, depths, fees, expected profit]. Return pass or abort with reasons.

output_examples.input: Trade size is 10,000 USDC and available pool depth near the current price is 22,000 USDC.
output_examples.output: Abort for low depth. The depth is 2.2 times trade size, below the 3 times rule.

output_examples.input: Expected output is 4.25 ETH with 100 basis points slippage tolerance.
output_examples.output: Minimum output is 4.2075 ETH. This uses expected output multiplied by 0.99.

output_examples.input: Route A has 35 bps impact and enough depth. Route B has 82 bps impact and enough depth.
output_examples.output: Prefer Route A. Route B exceeds the 50 basis point price impact limit.

best_practices: Provide current pool depth, trade size, expected output, and spot price in each request.
Check depth, price impact, and profit survival before considering execution.
Use conservative slippage limits when pool data is stale or volatile.
anti_patterns: Do not treat the skill output as a live market quote.
Do not execute a trade when any required input is missing.
Do not ignore price impact because expected profit appears large.

faq.question: Does this skill fetch live DEX liquidity?
faq.answer: No. It analyzes liquidity and slippage using data that you provide.

faq.question: Can it execute swaps?
faq.answer: No. It provides analysis and abort reasoning only.

faq.question: Which DEX types does it mention?
faq.answer: The source text mentions AMM math, Uniswap, and Curve as relevant contexts.

faq.question: What is the default price impact limit?
faq.answer: The skill uses 50 basis points as the stated maximum price impact.

faq.question: What is the default slippage limit?
faq.answer: The skill uses 100 basis points as the stated maximum slippage tolerance.

faq.question: Is this financial advice?
faq.answer: No. It is a calculation and review aid for liquidity and slippage checks.
